rice.name: Rice
rice.overview: Rice is a water-responsive cereal suited to warm, humid climates with reliable water availability. Suitability depends strongly on temperature, humidity, soil reaction (pH), and water balance alongside NPK status.
rice.irrigation: Where rainfall is insufficient, maintain shallow flooded or saturated soil during vegetative stages; reduce water depth toward maturity. Follow local extension schedules for your district—canal timing and water-release rules vary across southern states.
rice.water_note: High rainfall × humidity combinations often align with rice systems, but avoid waterlogging without drainage planning.

maize.name: Maize
maize.overview: Maize responds to balanced NPK and adequate soil moisture through the growing season; heat and moisture stress during flowering hurt yield.
maize.irrigation: Irrigate at knee-high, tasseling/silking, and grain filling if rains fail—split N applications per soil test where possible.

cotton.name: Cotton
cotton.overview: Cotton needs a long frost-free period and careful water management—too much rain at open boll can hurt fibre quality.
cotton.irrigation: Common approach: limited irrigation during squaring and peak bloom; avoid excess humidity where boll rot is prevalent.

mungbean.name: Mungbean (Green gram)
mungbean.overview: Short-duration legume; often grown with residual moisture or light irrigation. As a legume, it can contribute to nitrogen balance in rotation.
mungbean.irrigation: Light, infrequent irrigation if needed; avoid waterlogging.

blackgram.name: Black gram (Urad)
blackgram.overview: Warm-season pulse; sensitive to waterlogging. Often used in rice fallows in southern systems when drainage is adequate.
blackgram.irrigation: Minimal irrigation—only to establish or relieve moisture stress at flowering/pod fill.

pigeonpeas.name: Pigeon pea (Red gram / Tur)
pigeonpeas.overview: Deep-rooted pulse suited to semi-arid to sub-humid conditions; long-duration varieties need planned moisture through pod development.
pigeonpeas.irrigation: Often rainfed; one or two protective irrigations during flowering and pod filling if soil moisture collapses.
